content:
# Tasks Document

## References

- **Issue:** FORGE-34
- **Spec Path:** `.spec-workflow/specs/FORGE-34-stvault-import-export-upgrade/`

## File Touch Map

| File | Action | Scope |
|------|--------|-------|
| `src/types/index.ts` | MODIFY | Add `vulnDevices?`, `vulnScanCache?`, `preferences?`, `exportOptions?` to `VaultExportData`; add `ExportOptions` interface |
| `src/store/index.ts` | MODIFY | Update `exportData()` to include new fields with credential stripping; update `importData()` to handle new fields with merge logic |
| `src/components/VaultModal.tsx` | MODIFY | Export tab: category checkboxes, counts. Import tab: merge/replace toggle, category checkboxes, enhanced preview, re-setup notices |
| `src/__tests__/vault-roundtrip.test.ts` | CREATE | Roundtrip export/import tests for new fields, backward compat, selective options |

---

## VERSION CHECKOUT GATE

> Before starting ANY task, verify you are on the correct worktree branch (not `main`).
> ```bash
> git branch --show-current  # Must NOT be main
> ```
> If on `main`, STOP — create a worktree first via `/using-git-worktrees`.

---

- [ ] 1. Extend VaultExportData type and add ExportOptions interface
  - **File:** `src/types/index.ts` (MODIFY)
  - Add optional fields to `VaultExportData`: `vulnDevices?: VulnDevice[]`, `vulnScanCache?: Record<string, ScanEntry[]>`, `preferences?: Partial<Preferences>`, `exportOptions?: ExportOptions`
  - Add new `ExportOptions` interface with boolean flags: `includeGeneratedConfigs`, `includePlugins`, `includeVulnDevices`, `includeVulnScanCache`, `includePreferences`
  - Add `defaultExportOptions` constant with sensible defaults (all true except `includeVulnScanCache: false`)
  - Import `VulnDevice`, `ScanEntry` types — they're currently in `src/plugins/vuln-cisco/types.ts`
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/types/index.ts:162-172` (existing VaultExportData), `src/plugins/vuln-cisco/types.ts` (VulnDevice, ScanEntry types)_
  - _Requirements: R1 (complete data export), R2 (complete data import)_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-34-stvault-import-export-upgrade, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: TypeScript Developer | Task: Extend the VaultExportData interface at src/types/index.ts:162-172 to include three new optional fields for vuln scanner data, scan cache, and preferences. Add a new ExportOptions interface and defaultExportOptions constant. Import VulnDevice and ScanEntry types from src/plugins/vuln-cisco/types.ts. All new VaultExportData fields MUST be optional (?) for backward compatibility — old vault files that lack these fields must still parse without errors. | Restrictions: Do NOT modify existing fields. Do NOT change the VaultEnvelope interface. Keep the import path for VulnDevice/ScanEntry as a type-only import. | Success: TypeScript compiles without errors (`npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.app.json` — ignore pre-existing dompurify error). VaultExportData has 4 new optional fields. ExportOptions interface and defaultExportOptions constant are exported. After completion: mark task [-] → run log-implementation → mark [x]._

- [ ] 2. Update exportData() with new fields and credential stripping
  - **File:** `src/store/index.ts` (MODIFY — `exportData` function, ~line 974-1007)
  - Accept optional `ExportOptions` parameter (default to `defaultExportOptions`)
  - Add vuln device sanitization: map over `state.vulnDevices`, destructure out `snmpCommunity`
  - Add preferences filtering: destructure out `expandedNodes` from `state.preferences`
  - Conditionally include each new field based on `ExportOptions` flags
  - Include `exportOptions` metadata in the return object
  - Update the `exportData` type signature in the `ForgeStore` interface (~line 258)
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/store/index.ts:974-1007` (existing exportData with plugin sanitization pattern), `src/store/index.ts:258` (ForgeStore interface type signature), `src/types/index.ts` (ExportOptions from Task 1)_
  - _Requirements: R1.1 (vulnDevices with SNMP stripped), R1.2 (preferences minus expandedNodes), R1.3 (vulnScanCache opt-in), R1.4 (credential stripping pattern), R4.2 (exclude unchecked categories), R4.4 (item counts rely on exported data shape)_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-34-stvault-import-export-upgrade, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: React/Zustand Developer | Task: Update the exportData() action in src/store/index.ts (~line 974) to accept an optional ExportOptions parameter and include three new data types in the return object. Follow the EXISTING credential-stripping pattern (lines 980-997) for plugin secrets — apply the same approach to vuln device snmpCommunity fields (destructure them out). Filter preferences to exclude expandedNodes (node IDs are instance-specific). Conditionally include each field based on ExportOptions flags. Also update the ForgeStore interface type signature for exportData at ~line 258. | Restrictions: Do NOT change existing plugin sanitization logic. Do NOT change the function from synchronous to async. The defaultExportOptions import comes from src/types/index.ts (Task 1). | Success: exportData() returns vulnDevices (sans snmpCommunity), preferences (sans expandedNodes), and optionally vulnScanCache. TypeScript compiles. Existing tests still pass (`npm run test`). After completion: mark task [-] → run log-implementation → mark [x]._

- [ ] 3. Update importData() with new field handling
  - **File:** `src/store/index.ts` (MODIFY — `importData` function, ~line 870-971)
  - Add vuln device merge: iterate `data.vulnDevices`, skip if device ID exists, add new ones. If a device's `viewId` doesn't match any view in state, reassign to the first available view.
  - Add preferences merge: deep-merge `data.preferences` with current `state.preferences` (imported values win, current defaults fill gaps).
  - Add scan cache merge: for each `deviceId` in `data.vulnScanCache`, concatenate entries with existing cache.
  - All three sections guarded by `if (data.vulnDevices)` / `if (data.preferences)` / `if (data.vulnScanCache)` for backward compat.
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/store/index.ts:870-971` (existing importData merge logic — view merge, plugin merge with credential preservation), `src/store/index.ts:1190-1196` (existing vuln device viewId migration pattern in Zustand merge)_
  - _Requirements: R2.1 (merge vuln devices by ID), R2.2 (deep-merge preferences), R2.3 (additive scan cache merge), R2.4 (backward compat — missing fields graceful), R2.5 (orphaned viewId reassignment), R3.2 (SNMP re-entry notice data — devices without snmpCommunity)_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-34-stvault-import-export-upgrade, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: React/Zustand Developer | Task: Extend the importData() action in src/store/index.ts (~line 870) to handle three new optional fields from VaultExportData. For vulnDevices: iterate imported devices, skip any whose ID already exists in state.vulnDevices, add new ones. Check each imported device's viewId against available views — if no match, reassign to state.tree.views[0].id. For preferences: deep-merge with spread operator (imported values override current). For vulnScanCache: iterate entries, concatenate with existing cache per deviceId. All sections must be guarded with existence checks for backward compatibility. | Restrictions: Do NOT modify existing view/template/plugin merge logic. Do NOT change the importData type signature (it already accepts VaultExportData which now has the new optional fields from Task 1). | Success: importData() handles all three new fields. Old vault files (missing new fields) import without errors. TypeScript compiles. Existing tests pass. After completion: mark task [-] → run log-implementation → mark [x]._

- [ ] 4. Unit tests for vault export/import roundtrip
  - **File:** `src/__tests__/vault-roundtrip.test.ts` (CREATE)
  - Test 1: Export with all options enabled → import on clean state → verify all non-sensitive data matches (vuln devices present but snmpCommunity absent, preferences present but expandedNodes absent, scan cache present)
  - Test 2: Export with `includeVulnScanCache: false` → verify scan cache not in export
  - Test 3: Import vault file missing new fields (backward compat) → no errors, existing data unchanged
  - Test 4: Import vuln devices with orphaned viewId → verify reassigned to first view
  - Test 5: Selective ExportOptions — each flag independently toggled
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/__tests__/vault-engine.test.ts` (existing vault test patterns), `src/store/index.ts` (exportData, importData, resetAll), `src/types/index.ts` (VaultExportData, ExportOptions)_
  - _Requirements: R1 (complete export), R2 (complete import), R2.4 (backward compat), R2.5 (orphaned viewId)_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-34-stvault-import-export-upgrade, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Test Engineer | Task: Create src/__tests__/vault-roundtrip.test.ts with Vitest tests covering the vault export/import pipeline upgrade. Write 5 tests: (1) full roundtrip with all new fields, verifying sensitive data stripped; (2) scan cache opt-out via ExportOptions; (3) backward compat import of vault file missing new fields; (4) orphaned viewId reassignment; (5) selective ExportOptions flag toggling. Use the Zustand store directly — call exportData() and importData() on the store. | Restrictions: Do NOT test the vault encryption engine (that has its own test suite). Test the store's export/import logic only. Use Vitest (not Jest). Follow existing test patterns from src/__tests__/. | Success: All 5 tests pass (`npm run test`). Tests cover the key scenarios from requirements R1, R2. After completion: mark task [-] → run log-implementation → mark [x]._

- [ ] 5. VaultModal export tab — category checkboxes and item counts
  - **File:** `src/components/VaultModal.tsx` (MODIFY — export tab section, ~line 456-563)
  - Add `exportOptions` state using `ExportOptions` type with `defaultExportOptions` as initial value
  - Add checkbox group below the scope selector: Generated Configs (default on), Plugin Settings (on, note: "Credentials stripped"), Vuln Devices (on, note: "SNMP stripped"), Scan History (off, note: "May increase file size"), Preferences (on)
  - "Views & Templates" label above checkboxes — always included, no checkbox
  - Pass `exportOptions` to `exportData(exportOptions)` in `handleExport`
  - Add compact item count summary line below checkboxes (e.g., "3 views, 12 templates, 5 devices")
  - Read `UI-DESIGN-LANGUAGE.md` before writing any UI code
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/components/VaultModal.tsx:456-563` (existing export tab), `src/components/VaultModal.tsx:472-498` (scope selector pattern — radio buttons with labels), `UI-DESIGN-LANGUAGE.md` (MUST read before UI work)_
  - _Requirements: R4.1 (category checkboxes), R4.2 (unchecked excluded), R4.3 (scan history opt-in), R4.4 (item counts per category)_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-34-stvault-import-export-upgrade, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: React Frontend Developer | Task: Update the export tab in src/components/VaultModal.tsx to add category checkboxes for selective export. FIRST read UI-DESIGN-LANGUAGE.md to understand Forge's UI patterns. Add ExportOptions state with defaultExportOptions as initial value. Add a checkbox group below the existing scope selector with 5 categories: Generated Configs (default on), Plugin Settings (on, with "Credentials will be stripped" note), Vuln Devices (on, with "SNMP communities will be stripped" note), Scan History (off, with "May increase file size" note), Preferences (on). Use the existing scope selector as a visual pattern reference (bg-forge-obsidian, rounded-lg, text-sm). Add a compact summary line showing item counts. Pass exportOptions to exportData() in handleExport. | Restrictions: Do NOT modify the import tab (Task 6 handles that). Do NOT change the password input or encryption flow. Use existing Tailwind classes from the Forge design language — no new color tokens. Checkboxes use accent-amber-500 (matching existing radio pattern). | Success: Export tab shows category checkboxes. Unchecked categories are excluded from the exported vault file. Item counts display below checkboxes. TypeScript compiles. Visual style matches existing VaultModal patterns. After completion: mark task [-] → run log-implementation → mark [x]._

- [ ] 6. VaultModal import tab — merge/replace, category checkboxes, preview, re-setup notices
  - **File:** `src/components/VaultModal.tsx` (MODIFY — import tab section, ~line 566-770)
  - Add `importStrategy` state: `'merge' | 'replace'` (default: `'merge'`)
  - Add strategy toggle after decrypt, before conflict view: radio buttons for "Merge with existing" / "Replace all (erase current data first)"
  - When "Replace all" selected, show red-bordered confirmation warning
  - In `handleConfirmImport`: if strategy is 'replace', call `resetAll()` before `importData()`
  - Add import category checkboxes (one per data type present in vault file, all checked by default). Filter `decryptedData` before passing to `importData()`.
  - Expand `countData()` helper to include templates, generated configs, plugins, vuln devices, scan entries
  - Add re-setup notices (amber warning boxes): list plugins needing re-configuration, note SNMP community re-entry for vuln devices
  - Pull `resetAll` from `useForgeStore`
  - Read `UI-DESIGN-LANGUAGE.md` before writing any UI code
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/components/VaultModal.tsx:566-770` (existing import tab), `src/components/VaultModal.tsx:83-121` (countData helper), `src/components/VaultModal.tsx:612-645` (conflict resolution UI pattern), `src/store/index.ts:845-867` (resetAll action), `UI-DESIGN-LANGUAGE.md` (MUST read before UI work)_
  - _Requirements: R2.5 (orphaned viewId notice), R3.1 (plugin re-setup notice), R3.2 (SNMP re-entry notice), R5.1 (merge/replace toggle), R5.2 (replace confirmation warning), R5.3 (resetAll before import), R5.4 (import category checkboxes), R5.5 (unchecked excluded), R5.6 (existing conflict UI preserved), R6.1 (full category counts), R6.2 (plugin re-setup in preview), R6.3 (vuln device notice in preview)_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-34-stvault-import-export-upgrade, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: React Frontend Developer | Task: Update the import tab in src/components/VaultModal.tsx with merge/replace strategy, category checkboxes, enhanced preview, and re-setup notices. FIRST read UI-DESIGN-LANGUAGE.md. (1) Add importStrategy state ('merge'/'replace', default merge). Add radio toggle after decrypt. When 'replace' selected, show red-bordered warning "This will erase all existing data before importing." (2) In handleConfirmImport, if strategy is 'replace', call resetAll() (pull from useForgeStore) before importData(). (3) Add category checkboxes for each data type present in the decrypted vault file (all checked by default). Filter decryptedData by unchecking before passing to importData(). (4) Expand countData() to include counts for templates, generated configs, plugins, vuln devices, scan entries — not just views/vendors/models/variants. (5) Add amber warning boxes for re-setup notices: if plugins present, list plugin names needing re-configuration; if vuln devices present, note SNMP community re-entry needed. | Restrictions: Do NOT modify the export tab (Task 5 handles that). Preserve existing conflict resolution UI (skip/overwrite/rename). Use existing Forge Tailwind classes. The "Replace all" confirmation must require a deliberate click — not just selecting the radio button. | Success: Import tab shows merge/replace toggle, category checkboxes, full-category preview counts, and re-setup notices. "Replace all" calls resetAll() before importData(). Existing conflict resolution still works. TypeScript compiles. After completion: mark task [-] → run log-implementation → mark [x]._

---

## Standard Closing Tasks

- [ ] 7. Final integration verification
  - Run full test suite: `npm run test`
  - Run type check: `npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.app.json`
  - Run lint: `npm run lint`
  - Manual smoke test: export → wipe localStorage → import → verify all data types restored
  - _Prompt: Role: QA Engineer | Task: Run the full verification suite for FORGE-34. Execute npm run test, npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.app.json (ignore dompurify error), and npm run lint. Then manually verify: open dev server, create test data (view, vendor, model, variant, vuln device), export vault, clear localStorage, import vault, verify all data restored (except stripped credentials). Log results. | Success: All tests pass. Type check passes (minus known dompurify). Lint passes. Manual roundtrip confirms data integrity._
